english: cruel, fierce, severe, lha gnyán-rnams (lha gnyan rnams) Glr. gods of vengeance, deities of terror; klu-gnyán (klu gnyan) id.; krims gnyán-pa (khrims gnyan pa) a cruel commandment, frq.; dam-tsig gnyán-pa (dam tshig gnyan pa) prob. a rigid vow, a solemn oath Mil.; of mountains: wild, rugged, precipitous; gnyán-sa (gnyan sa) a rugged country Mil.; in gnyán-pai gnad (gnyan pa'i gnad) (v. gnad (gnad)) prob.: dangerous. - gnyán-po (gnyan po) sbst. Mil.?

1. neck, nape, gnyá-ba brtuns (gnya' ba brtungs) the neck is contracted or shortened Med. - gnyá-ko (gnya' ko) hide, or leather of a beast's neck Cs. - gnya-kóbs (gnya' kobs) screen of the neck (attached to a helmet) Sch. - gnya-rgyáb (gnya' rgyab) (?) C. breast-work, parapet. - gnya-rtsé (gnya' rtse) vertebra prominens, the cervical vertebra with its projecting process Mil. - gnya-tsígs (gnya' tshigs) cervical joint. - gnya-réṅs (gnya' rengs) stiff neck, gnya-réṅs-cán (gnya' rengs can) 1. having a stiff neck; 2. stiffnecked, obstinate. - gnya-śiṅ (gnya' shing) a yoke (for oxen) Glr., Lex. - 2. skad-gnyá (skad gnya') v. skad (skad).
or snya-náṅ (snya nang), a village on the frontier of Nepal

a witness, one that gives evidence Cs., Lex. = dpáṅ-po (dpang po); gnyá-bo byéd-pa (gnya' bo byed pa) to pledge for, to be surety for; Dzl. W: bskyi-gnyá byas (bskyi gnya' byas), Sch.: 'he made an attested loan'.
for gnyis-ka (gnyis ka) Stg.; gnyi-zér (gnyi zer) for nyi-zér (nyi zer) Lex.

resp. mnal (mnal), sleep, gnyid-du gró-ba (gnyid du 'gro ba) to fall asleep Glr., Mil.; W. *gnyid ma yoṅ* (gnyid ma yong) sleep has not come, I am sleepless; *gnyid ma kug, gnyid kug ma nyan* (gnyid ma khug, gnyid khug ma nyan), also *gnyid saṅ soṅ* (gnyid sang song) id.; gnyid mi tub (gnyid mi thub) he cannot find sleep Med.; gnyid-túm-pa (gnyid thum pa) one uninterrupted portion of sleep Glr.; gnyid mtúg-pa (gnyid mthug pa) a sound sleep, gnyid-sráb (gnyid srab) a light sleep, a slumber Med. - gnyid-log-pa (gnyid log pa) (prop. gnyid-kyis lóg-pa (gnyid kyis log pa)) Dzl. to fall asleep, Dzl. 172, 16; Dzl. 9 (thus correctly translated already by Schr.), prob. also to sleep; gnyid-la gró-ba (gnyid la 'gro ba), W. *ča-če* (cha che), to fall asleep; gnyid túg-por soṅ (gnyid thug por song) he fell into a deep sleep Mil.; *da-rúṅ gnyid ma lóg-mkan-dug* (da rung gnyid ma log mkhan 'dug) W. I am still awake; gnyid sád-pa (gnyid sad pa) to awaken, to awake vb. n.; gnyid-yúr-ba (gnyid yur ba) to be overcome by sleep Sch., Tar. 31, 22, Pth., - gnyid-rdól (gnyid rdol) C. somnambulism; *gnyid-ma-mún-la dúl-če* (gnyid ma mun la 'dul ce) id., Ld.; *gnyi'-čól gyáb-pa* (gnyi' chol rgyab pa) id. C. - gnyid-cán (gnyid can) sleepy Cs.; gnyid-méd (gnyid med) having no sleep, sleepless; gnyid-yér (gnyid yer) morbid sleeplessness; gnyid-yár (gnyid yar) Med., Pth., id? gnyid-lam (gnyid lam) C. = rmi-lam (rmi lam) dream.

1. also gnyis śig (gnyis shig) (v. čig (chig)), two, de gnyis (de gnyis), gnyis-po (gnyis po), gnyis-ka (gnyis ka) the two, both; gnyis(-su)-méd(-pa) (gnyis su med pa); mi-gnyis-pa (mi gnyis pa) Tar., not being two, i.e. not differing, identical, the same, ṅa daṅ rgyál-ba gnyis-su med (nga dang rgyal ba gnyis su med) I and Buddha, we are one, i.e. I am an incarnation of Buddha Glr.; Cs. also: indubitable, thus perh. used by Mil.; gnyis-su byúṅ-ba (gnyis su byung ba) to be divided into two, to become two Glr. - 2. a (married) couple, brám-ze gnyis (bram ze gnyis) Brahmin man and wife. - 3. both (v. above), in Tibetan often added, where two nouns have the same predicate, either disjunctively, and then usually followed by re (re): jó-bo daṅ byams-mgón gnyis mdzó-mo reï stéṅ-du bźugs (jo bo dang byams mgon gnyis mdzo mo re'i steng du bzhugs) both the lord and the Maitreya were mounted on bastard-cows Glr.; ṅa-ráṅ re gnyis (nga rang re gnyis) either of us Mil.; phyi naṅ gnyis čós-lugs gaṅ bzaṅ (phyi nang gnyis chos lugs gang bzang) which is the better of the two religions, the esoteric, or the exoteric? Glr.; - or copulatively: kyo-śúg gnyis-la rás-čug gčig-las mi bdóg-ste (khyo shug gnyis la ras chug gcig las mi bdog ste) as they both, husband and wife, had only one cloth together Dzl.; - and reciprocally: čos daṅ bon gnyis rtsód-pa (chos dang bon gnyis rtsod pa) the contest between the religion of Buddha, and the religion of the Bons Glr.; kyod daṅ ṅa gnyis bza-mí byao (khyod dang nga gnyis bza' mi bya'o) we two shall marry each other Glr. In most cases mentioned sub 3, gnyis-po (gnyis po) (the two), gnyis-ka (gnyis ka), (g)nyi-ga ((g)nyi ga), W. col. also *nyi-ko, nyi-kad, nyi-kod* (nyi ko, nyi kad, nyi kod), Sp. *nyi-mo* (nyi mo), may be used inst. of gnyis (gnyis); gnyis (gnyis) may also refer to several nouns on one or on both sides: kyed daṅ ṅa gnyis (khyed dang nga gnyis) both you (referring to several persons) and I; but it may also be quite omitted, as in other languages: ga daṅ bai jug-tsúl (ga dang ba'i 'jug tshul) the way
